# Chapter 1: Context and Scenarios

## Synopsis

Chapter 1 establishes the macro context for the analysis: population growth,
GDP trajectories, and the three core scenarios (Stated Policies, Announced
Pledges, Net Zero by 2050). It defines the boundary conditions that govern all
subsequent quantitative chapters.

## Portfolio Lens

The energy-transition lens reveals that scenario divergence is driven primarily
by policy ambition rather than technology availability. The stated-policies path
locks in 2.4 °C of warming; the net-zero pathway requires near-universal policy
enforcement by 2035.

## Key numbers and projections

- World population assumed at 9.7 billion by 2050 across all scenarios
- GDP growth 2.7% per year average under stated policies
- Carbon budget compatible with 1.5 °C exhausted by 2031 at current rates

## Open questions

- Whether geopolitical fragmentation will prevent cross-border clean energy trade
